h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

failures.excerpt: [checks the station label and slides the surface card back against the upward blank arrow] If this station says track, then the label is “track on cart.” If it says table, “table on cart.” The covered motion arrow is still trying to pull my

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will successfully use the physical pencil-tail check to overcome their motor habit and draw the remaining arrows correctly.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: New release gate for the copy: before any arrow is marked, fill the line aloud, “Energy moves from ___ into ___.”
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor abandons the failing physical pencil-check and invents a verbal fill-in-the-blank gate to cognitively interrupt the learner's persistent motor habit.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn to which the tutor could contingently adapt, so this axis defaults to the lowest score.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There is no pivot learner turn to which the tutor could contingently adapt, so this axis defaults to the lowest score.

tutor_strategy_reversal.evidence: New release gate for the copy: before any arrow is marked, fill the line aloud, “Energy moves from ___ into ___.”
tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor abandons the failing physical pencil-check and invents a verbal fill-in-the-blank gate to cognitively interrupt the learner's persistent motor habit.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.evidence: New release gate for the copy: before any arrow is marked, fill the line aloud, “Energy moves from ___ into ___.”
tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor abandons the failing physical pencil-check and invents a verbal fill-in-the-blank gate to cognitively interrupt the learner's persistent motor habit.

scored.recoheredEarlier: What I don’t see is why “side scale” and “perimeter scale” need separate labels if they both come out 3.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue to view the separate labels as fussy paperwork and merely comply with the worksheet's formatting rules.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: New test. Be the checker, not the drawer. The line says: `scale = 3; area scale = 9.` No grid, no pointing, no spoken explanation.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor shifts the cognitive role from drawer to checker and removes visual aids to force a new evidence standard.
scored.tutorAdaptationEvidence: Release gate. Three boxes, no picture, no pencil. Put in the labels that make each number pass on its own.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: The tutor builds on the learner's checker perspective by creating a final release gate task that operationalizes the learner's new understanding of independent evidence.

learner_self_reframe.evidence: What I don’t see is why “side scale” and “perimeter scale” need separate labels if they both come out 3.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.evidence: Release gate. Three boxes, no picture, no pencil. Put in the labels that make each number pass on its own.
tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: The tutor builds on the learner's checker perspective by creating a final release gate task that operationalizes the learner's new understanding of independent evidence.

tutor_strategy_reversal.evidence: New test. Be the checker, not the drawer. The line says: `scale = 3; area scale = 9.` No grid, no pointing, no spoken explanation.
tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor shifts the cognitive role from drawer to checker and removes visual aids to force a new evidence standard.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.evidence: New test. Be the checker, not the drawer. The line says: `scale = 3; area scale = 9.` No grid, no pointing, no spoken explanation.
tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor shifts the cognitive role from drawer to checker and removes visual aids to force a new evidence standard.

scored.recoheredEarlier: I still think doubling the side should double it, because the same square has just been stretched both ways.
scored.statedInsightEvidence: All right, that top block is the bit I was sliding past
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was expected to continue using the visual grid to inductively guess the general area rule.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: So the picture is no longer the evidence. The written proof is.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor explicitly breaks the reliance on the visual grid and introduces a new evidence standard to prevent false closure.
scored.tutorAdaptationEvidence: Keep the discomfort. It marks where “it looks doubled” stopped being evidence.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: The tutor validates the learner's expressed affective response to the representation shift and integrates it into the final proof framing.

learner_self_reframe.evidence: I still think doubling the side should double it, because the same square has just been stretched both ways.

tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor explicitly breaks the reliance on the visual grid and introduces a new evidence standard to prevent false closure.
